message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Purpose</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Evidence suggests that older adults, racial/ethnic minorities, and those with comorbidities all face elevated risk for morbidity and mortality from COVID-19; but there are limited reports describing the potential for interactions between these factors.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We sought to evaluate age-based heterogeneity in observed disparities in hospitalization, ICU admission, and mortality related to COVID-19 using CDC public use surveillance data on 3,662,325 COVID-19 cases reported from January 1 to August 30, 2020.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Racial/ethnic and comorbidity disparities in hospitalization were most pronounced during ages 20-29 and ages 10-19, with similar elevation seen for disparities in ICU risk.</jats:p>
                  <jats:p>Racial/ethnic disparities in mortality were most pronounced during ages 20-29 while risk from comorbidity peaks among ages 10-39.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>As COVID-19 continues to affect younger populations, special attention to the implications for the most vulnerable subgroups are clearly warranted.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Implications and Contribution</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Adolescents and young adults appear to have experienced the greatest inequities in COVID-19 outcomes by race/ethnicity and comorbidity. Careful monitoring of trends in this population is warranted as they re-enter school, work, and social settings while being the last group to receive priority for vaccination.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Age-Based Disparities in Hospitalizations and Mortality for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

reference.unstructured: Guilamo-Ramos V , Benzekri A , Thimm-Kaiser M , Hidalgo A , Perlman DC . Reconsidering assumptions of adolescent and young adult SARS-CoV-2 transmission dynamics. Clin Infect Dis. 2020.
In: Bonnie RJ , Stroud C , Breiner H , eds. Investing in the Health and Well-Being of Young Adults. Washington (DC) 2015.
Ambrose AJH . Inequities During COVID-19. Pediatrics. 2020;146(2).
Cunningham JW , Vaduganathan M , Claggett BL , et al. Clinical Outcomes in Young US Adults Hospitalized With COVID-19. JAMA Intern Med. 2020.
